
---
title: "Welch's t-test Mean Agility of Left Forwards vs Left Midfielders"
author: "Isabella Westlauer"
date: "1/31/2026"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
# Load the ggplot2 library
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Define parameters and calculate critical values
mu <- 0
sigma <- 1
alpha <- 0.05
df <- 14   # placeholder df for Welch t-test illustration

t_critical_left  <- qt(alpha / 2, df = df)
t_critical_right <- qt(1 - alpha / 2, df = df)

# z_critical_left <- qnorm(alpha / 2, mean = mu, sd = sigma)
# z_critical_right <- qnorm(1 - alpha / 2, mean = mu, sd = sigma)

# 2. Create a data frame for plotting (ggplot requires a data frame)
plot_data <- data.frame(x = seq(-4, 4, length.out = 1000))

# 3. Create the base plot with the normal curve
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = x)) +
  stat_function(
    fun = dt,
    args = list(df = df),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Sampling Distribution of X̄_LM − X̄_LF (α = 0.05)",
    x = expression(bar(X)[LM] - bar(X)[LF]),
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 4. Add shaded critical regions, left and right, using 'geom_area'
p <- p +
  geom_area(
    data = subset(plot_data, x < t_critical_left),
    aes(y = dt(x, df)),
    fill = "red",
    alpha = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_area(
    data = subset(plot_data, x > t_critical_right),
    aes(y = dt(x, df)),
    fill = "red",
    alpha = 0.4
  )

# 5. Add vertical lines for the critical values
p <- p +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = c(t_critical_left, t_critical_right),
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = c(t_critical_left, t_critical_right),
    y = 0.02,
    label = c(expression(-t^"*"), expression(t^"*")),
    hjust = c(1.2, -0.2)
  )

t_obs <- 1.810906

p <- p +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = t_obs,
    color = "blue",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = t_obs,
    y = 0.35,
    label = expression(t[obs]),
    hjust = -0.1,
    color = "blue"
  )


# Print the plot
print(p)
```
